item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After re-reading, I found out what bothers me here that make it doesn't seem too interesting.

The basic concept is an anomaly that needs, by its own nature, to be well treated instead of coerced into submission to be contained. That is pretty much a fair concept.

But the execution is through defining that a specific DCL is needed for the containment to be enacted, but the containment can be done at the Foundation site, and Foundation personnel here is basically assumed as incapable of acting with the needed kindness toward the animal.

It does strike me as both a bit of LOL Foundation, and overall a weird change of focus on containment procedures. I think first that it should be contained at WWS under supervision (given its potential to cause damage if the very search is not properly handled in case of a containment breach), instead of at a Foundation site, since WWS does have facilities specialized in keeping animals happy and healthy, while their best efforts won't change that the Foundation keep their animals on underground facilities with limited space (that is the description of Site-64).

You can then have the re-containment limited to personnel trained by the DCL, instead of only the DCL herself, with makes little sense. It can even be established that WWS personnel is eligible for this training, and that the DCL is responsible for leading and coordinating re-containment if it comes to be needed.

Overall the concept is good, but making it seem like Foundation personnel can't be good to an animal if their lives depended on it was awkward, and you lost an opportunity to place this one under WWS care (no need to turn this into a Critter Profile if it is supervised). It will be a downvote for now, but it can easily turn into an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think the reference to the Boring Agreement in the last paragraph was a good choice of words. First because it seems like an odd place to put it, second because it doesn't sound like the Agreement helped in containment, it sounds like the Agreement defined containment for this situation, that is, it wouldn't be considered containment to have the bird inside a closed aviary otherwise.

Maybe you should put the reference to the Agreement in the Containment session, since it explains why the WWS employee is accepted as a liaison.

Otherwise, I don't know. It seems like a no-vote for me, but I'll take another look just to be sure. Not a great addition to the site, I felt nothing, but still good enough to be another random anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fully functioning, non-tampered-with SCRAMBLE goggles were used in the newer SCP-1730 logs, and somewhere else I can't recall offhand. There, they were described as able to block out a wide range of cognitohazards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed the article and the anomaly, as well as the compassion from WWS. It's a nice change of pace for most SCP articles. I'm a little confused by the use of SCRAMBLE goggles, because correct me if I'm wrong, but weren't they both specifically programmed for SCP-096 and a failure? If there's documentation including SCRAMBLE gear besides Incident 096-A, then I'm probably wrong. Still a good article though. Upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. This is such a great little canon and it's always nice to see a collaborative Foundation with local roots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. I was too harsh earlier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A couple points. This is the only WWS where you could say "The Foundation is dumb", and only by stretching.

WWS skips so far:
– hide block

3466: WWS already has the situation handled, mostly.

3153: Mostly a Wilson's namedrop.

3465: Rogue Foundation individuals. Neither group as a whole acts "dumb".

3160: WWS needs the Foundation's help.

3577: WWS seeks Foundation help, problem solved via a single WWS employee using an unintuitive solution.

Even so. This GoI has only existed for 40 days. You know what the great thing about GOIs is? They can evolve.
This GOI is brand new and has plenty of time to explore its niche and Foundation-relationship. Compare WWS to something like GAW, where it took over a year before the first article appeared portraying the group in a less than positive light.

Has anyone done a story where WWS is wrong, and it blows up yet?

No, but it's very early on, and I'd welcome such an article! Might even write it myself…

Certainly, the Foundation would have caught the bird on their second try. Wilson's just saved them a trip. I just don't want you to discount the whole GOI because you disliked my one article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm starting to get tired of Wilson's Wildlife Solutions always being right, and the foundation looking dumb. Compare this to SCP-2145, where the foundation overcomes and captures an even more annoying quarry.

WWS is sucking the mercy out of the foundation. In previous articles, the foundation was capable of mercy, or of recognizing mercy as another tool towards containment. Stories are made interesting by conflict, and if WWS's main thing is mercy, then the foundation must be anti-mercy in these stories.

I'm just getting back into this, Has anyone done a story where WWS is wrong, and it blows up yet? That fits a wider view of the SCP-verse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is another solid article that gives Wilson's Wildlife Solutions a legitimate reason to exist, acting as a good cop to the Foundation's bad cop that enables it to contain anomalies with a drop of honey rather than a gallon of gall.

item.title: what is it with me and extinct animals
item.content:encoded:
Alright, here we go again. With this skip, Wilson's Wildlife Solutions now fulfills the requirements to be an official GOI! Let's give DarkStuff a big round of applause!

Thanks to DarkStuff, 9Volt does not match any existing user name, Captain Kirby, CadaverCommander, and Blooper for all their help.

rejected titles
not really

The Moa You Know
Moa Less
Moa Power to You
Moa? Na.
MTF AGENT Hurt Itself In Its Confusion!
Birdbrained
No Wings, No Problem


all hail Peggy Marquez, Conqueror of the Bird of War #marquezfamily2018

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Model_of_Giant_Moa%27s_Head.jpg CC 4.0 edited by hippo, thanks!
